title: Artemis 3 commander confident crew will be ready for 2027 mission
summary: The commander of NASA's Artemis 3 mission expressed confidence that the crew will be prepared for the mission, which is scheduled for 2027. This mission is considered one of NASA's most complex undertakings.

title: House appropriators back $55.5 billion Space Force budget, omit reconciliation funds
summary: House appropriators approved a $55.5 billion budget for the Space Force but excluded $350 billion in proposed reconciliation funds, raising concerns for space programs like Golden Dome.

title: Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes Two New Space Supply Chain Members
summary: The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) has welcomed Besxar and Charter Space as new members specializing in space-based semiconductor manufacturing. This addition expands the CSF's supply chain network within the commercial space industry.

title: Spire to pursue space-based missile warning in partnership with German defense firm
summary: Spire is partnering with German defense firm Diehl Defence to explore using its satellite constellation for detecting ballistic and hypersonic missile threats. This collaboration aims to advance space-based missile warning capabilities.

title: UK startup Applied Atomics to enter US market with focus on military space mobility
summary: UK startup Applied Atomics is expanding into the US market with a focus on military space mobility. The company has opened an office in Virginia and announced $4 million in pre-seed funding.

title: Orbital raises $5 million to join orbital data center race
summary: Orbital, a Los Angeles-based startup, raised $5 million to fund an in-orbit computing demonstration next year. The company plans to deploy over 100,000 orbital data centers to meet the growing demand for AI infrastructure.

title: LeoLabs deploys mobile space-tracking radar in Indo-Pacific
summary: LeoLabs has deployed a mobile space-tracking radar in the Indo-Pacific region to continuously monitor maneuvering spacecraft in orbit. This transportable sensor enhances space situational awareness and tracking capabilities.

title: Arkadia Space to supply thrusters for Reflex Aerospace satellite
summary: Arkadia Space has signed a deal to supply thrusters for a satellite being built by Reflex Aerospace. This collaboration highlights the growing role of propulsion startups in supporting satellite manufacturers.

title: Spaceport crunch reviving interest in sea-based launch
summary: The growing congestion at traditional spaceports is driving renewed interest in sea-based rocket launches. Offshore launches are seen as a solution to address both launch site limitations and military resilience needs.

title: After the launch: Why Europe’s space ambitions depend on what comes next
summary: Europe's space ambitions hinge on post-launch developments, as satellites are critical for financial, military, and logistical infrastructure. The success of these efforts depends on future advancements and deployment.

title: James Webb Space Telescope finds evidence the mysterious 'little red dots' are black hole stars
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has found evidence suggesting that 'little red dots' in the early universe may be black hole stars. This discovery could help astronomers understand the nature of these mysterious objects.

title: NASA chief defends all-male Artemis 3 astronaut crew amid backlash: 'I don't think anyone should be reading into this'
summary: NASA chief defended the all-male Artemis 3 astronaut crew selection, stating it was based on qualifications, despite public backlash. Officials emphasized that no one should overanalyze the decision.

title: 'Doctor Who' Christmas Special is cancelled as Russell T. Davies departs the show: What does this mean for the future of Doctor Who?
summary: The 'Doctor Who' Christmas Special has been cancelled following Russell T. Davies' departure from the show. This raises questions about the future direction of the long-running series.

title: Jupiter and Venus looked spectacular in this week's planetary conjunction. Here are our favorite photos
summary: Venus and Jupiter appeared close together in a planetary conjunction this week, creating a stunning visual spectacle for stargazers. The event provided an opportunity for breathtaking astronomical photography.

title: NASA's ambitious Artemis 3 mission includes 3 giant rocket launches, 2 private moon landers and 1 big question: Can it all work together?
summary: NASA's Artemis 3 mission in 2027 involves three rocket launches and two private moon landers, raising questions about coordination. The mission is one of the agency's most complex to date.

title: Gears of War E-Day developer The Coalition explains why the prequel was 'just too good to pass up'
summary: The Coalition, the developer behind Gears of War E-Day, discussed why creating the prequel was an irresistible opportunity during Summer Games Fest. The team shared insights into the latest chapter of the Gears of War saga.

title: Swirling spiral galaxy will hypnotize you | Space photo of the day for June 10, 2026
summary: The Hubble Space Telescope captured a stunning image of the Messier 88 (M88) galaxy, showcasing its swirling spiral structure. This galaxy, also known as NGC 4501, is featured as the Space Photo of the Day for June 10, 2026.

title: NASA is building a new space telescope to search for life on nearby planets. What would it see on ancient Earth?
summary: NASA is developing the Habitable Worlds Observatory to search for life on nearby planets. A study suggests it could detect biosignatures in the atmosphere of an ancient Earth-like exoplanet.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This milestone marks progress in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit (LEO). This crewed mission is part of NASA's Artemis program aimed at returning humans to the Moon.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase teams have begun cryogenic testing on Booster 20 in preparation for Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: Launch Preview: Chinese, Rocket Lab, and Starlink flights scheduled
summary: The upcoming week will feature multiple space launches, including three Starlink missions, flights from China, Rocket Lab, and launches from California, Virginia, and Florida.

title: NASA concludes MAVEN mission at Mars, uses data to discover new atmospheric phenomenon
summary: NASA has concluded the MAVEN mission at Mars after a six-month effort to restore communication. The mission's data has led to the discovery of a new atmospheric phenomenon.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its Blue Moon lunar lander and Mars missions despite the recent New Glenn rocket anomaly during hotfire testing at Launch Complex 36.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in developing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This advancement is a key milestone for the spacecraft's reusability and future missions.

title: Space Force evaluating anomaly-related damage, future danger areas
summary: Space Launch Delta 45 is assessing damage and potential future risks following an anomaly. The Eastern Range operations are under evaluation to determine the impact and necessary precautions.

title: Artemis III’s remaining solid rocket booster segments shipped from Utah to Florida
summary: The remaining solid rocket booster segments for Artemis III have been transported from Utah to Florida. This marks a key step in preparation for the mission, despite ongoing uncertainties about timelines and landers.

title: Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026
summary: Blue Origin has begun inspection, repair, and reconstruction activities at Launch Complex-36, aiming for a return-to-flight (RTF) by the end of 2026.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: No specific news content was provided for analysis.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication showcases key milestones in European space exploration and innovation.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses. This marks a return to routine science operations for the mission.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. This initiative supports European space science by involving research institutes in space missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance for the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to the European Space Agency's missions by testing and ensuring materials can withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section supports these efforts with innovative ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: Symphony Space Unveils Adagio-XL ODC Sat
summary: Symphony Space has introduced the Adagio-XL ODC satellite, marking its entry into the orbital data center market. The Adagio-XL is designed to provide data processing and storage capabilities in space.

title: Orbital Raises $5M Pre-Seed to Build ODC Constellation
summary: Orbital secures $5 million in pre-seed funding to develop its ODC constellation, which uses smaller satellites to handle large AI inference workloads and maintain constant ground contact.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo More Time to Launch
summary: The FCC has extended the deadline for Amazon's Project Kuiper to deploy at least half of its satellite constellation, while imposing conditions to expedite launches.

title: Artemis III Astronauts Will Meet SpaceX, Blue Origin Landers in Earth Orbit
summary: NASA confirmed that SpaceX and Blue Origin will each provide a lander for the Artemis III mission's 2027 Earth orbit test, paving the way for future Moon landings. The event marked a key step in NASA's lunar exploration plans.

title: Reflex Aerospace Taps Arkadia Space for 2027 Mission
summary: Reflex Aerospace has chosen Arkadia Space, a Spanish startup, to supply a propulsion system for its upcoming satellite mission scheduled for launch in 2027.

title: ICEYE, Isar Aerospace Announce Significant Funding Rounds
summary: ICEYE, a Finnish Earth observation company, raised €450M ($520M) in a Series F funding round at a €10B ($12B) valuation. Isar Aerospace was also mentioned in the funding announcement.

title: Quantum Space To Go Public Via SPAC
summary: Quantum Space announced plans to go public via a SPAC merger to secure capital for scaling and access to public markets. The company emphasized the urgency of speed in its strategic move.

title: NewOrbit Raises $18.5M to Build VLEO Spacecraft at Scale
summary: NewOrbit has raised $18.5 million to develop and scale VLEO-capable spacecraft. The company plans to launch its first NEO-1 satellite in 2028.

title: Juno Propulsion Raises $1.4M Pre-Seed for RDE Flight
summary: Juno Propulsion secured $1.4M in pre-seed funding to develop its first flight-ready rotating detonation engine (RDE), Project Iris, with a demo mission planned for 2027.

title: Mass Is the Easy Part, Coordination Is The Hard Part
summary: Ukraine's use of cheap drones has forced the U.S. to shift its defense strategy toward mass-produced, autonomous, and disposable systems. This marks a significant change in military procurement and tactics.

title: Another Earth Expands Into Brazil
summary: Another Earth, an Earth Observation data company, is expanding its operations into Brazil, citing the country's significant climate pressures and rapid growth. The move marks the company's first major expansion in Latin America.

title: Airbus to Lead German Consortium for Sovereign ISR Capability
summary: Airbus is leading a German consortium to develop a sovereign intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) solution. The announcement was made during the Berlin Air Show.

title: Sky Perfect JSAT to Purchase Avanti’s HYLAS 3 Satellite
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT will acquire Avanti's HYLAS 3 satellite currently in orbit. The satellite will undergo in-orbit testing after the purchase.

title: LeoLabs Deploys First Mobile Tracking Radar
summary: LeoLabs has deployed its first mobile space domain awareness (SDA) tracking radar in the Asia-Pacific region, housed in a shipping container. The system, called Scout-S, marks a significant expansion of its tracking capabilities.

title: 40 People Who Shaped the Satellite Industry: Part One
summary: Via Satellite celebrates its 40-year anniversary in 2026 by highlighting 40 influential individuals who shaped the satellite industry. The feature honors both past and present visionaries in the field.

title: Space Force Eyes Nearly $1.5B for Space Data Network Backbone in Future Reconciliation Bill
summary: The U.S. Space Force plans to request nearly $1.5 billion for the Space Data Network (SDN) Backbone in a future fiscal 2027 reconciliation bill. This funding aims to enhance the military's space data infrastructure.

title: Iceye’s Valuation Soars to 10 Billion Euro With New Raise
summary: Iceye's valuation surged to 10 billion euros ($12 billion) following a new funding round led by General Atlantic, raising 450 million euros ($520 million).

title: New Astroscale Initiative Looks to Improve Data for Spacecraft Reentry Studies
summary: Astroscale is launching a new initiative to enhance spacecraft reentry studies while safeguarding sensitive data for commercial operators. The company will lead the Atmospheric Impact project to improve data collection and analysis.

title: NRO Awards BlackSky Multi-Million Dollar Modification to Accelerate AROS Development
summary: The National Reconnaissance Office (NRO) awarded BlackSky a multi-million dollar contract modification to accelerate development of its AROS multi-spectral Earth observation satellites. This move aims to enhance broad area Earth observation capabilities.

title: Integrasys and Safran Electronics & Defense Invest in New German Facilities
summary: Integrasys and Safran Electronics & Defense are investing in new facilities in Germany, a key market for space and defense. The expansion aims to strengthen their presence in the region.
